summary: Manifest-only PR updating configure.py's MatchingFor object list to restore several item translation units from NonMatching to Matching after PR#2203, with one listed item object entry removed. The available bot report shows linked-code and linked-data percentage increases, but the PR body and review discussion provide no extra rationale.

key_files.role: Only changed file; updates item object Matching/NonMatching declarations inside MatchingFor.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py MatchingFor hunks around lines 1108-1241

what_changed.text: Changed item objects from NonMatching to Matching for melee/it/items/itheart.c, ittomato.c, itbox.c, itharisen.c, itlipstick.c, itmariocape.c, itchicorita.c, itthunder.c, and itfushigibana.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py replacements from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...)

what_changed.text: Removed the configure.py object entry for melee/it/items/itchicoritaleaf.c from the Pokémon item list in the shown diff, with no replacement line in the PR slice.
The PR title identifies the change as restoring matches removed by PR#2203.
A decomp bot issue comment reported linked code 31.03% (+0.41%, +15772 bytes) and linked data 28.88% (+0.08%, +1008 bytes), followed by 'No changes'.

smart_moves.text: Kept the restoration narrowly scoped to configure.py match-status declarations rather than mixing source rewrites with manifest repair.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: all hunks are Object(Matching/NonMatching) list edits

smart_moves.text: Used the project object manifest to restore multiple item TU match statuses in one small PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: multiple melee/it/items Object entries changed to Matching

decomp_lessons.lesson: Match status can be lost through configure.py object-list churn even when source files are unchanged; a focused manifest-only PR can restore known Matching objects.
evidence_refs: pr title: Restore matches removed by PR#2203

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For object-level matching progress, verify that configure.py's MatchingFor declarations still classify completed translation units as Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: changed object classifications for item files from NonMatching to Matching
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The slice does not include object diff output or PR#2203 details, so this is a manifest-maintenance tactic rather than evidence about C source reconstruction.

follow_up_queries: configure.py melee/it/items itchicoritaleaf object entry removed
itheart ittomato itbox itharisen itlipstick itmariocape itchicorita itthunder itfushigibana MatchingFor
decomp-dev bot GALE01 linked code +15772 PR 2213

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata and changed-file counts
text_corpus: empty PR body and one decomp-dev bot issue comment
notes: The changed file and exact manifest edits are strongly evidenced by the complete raw diff.
The stated reason, restoring matches removed by PR#2203, is supported only by the PR title; no PR body explains the prior regression.
There are no review comments or human review notes in the slice.
The bot report gives linked-code and linked-data deltas, but the slice does not include build logs, objdiff details, or per-object verification.
The removal of itchicoritaleaf.c from configure.py is visible in the diff, but the slice does not explain whether that removal is intentional, a duplicate/split correction, or related to PR#2203.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Configure manifest churn can remove existing matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR#2213 is a manifest-only repair in configure.py that restores several melee/it/items translation units from NonMatching to Matching after matches were reportedly removed by PR#2203. When matching progress unexpectedly drops, inspect MatchingFor object classification changes in configure.py in addition to source changes.
evidence_refs: pr title: Restore matches removed by PR#2203

source_update_candidates.title: PR#2213 configure.py item object match-status restoration
source_update_candidates.text: In PR#2213, configure.py's MatchingFor list changed the following item paths from NonMatching to Matching: melee/it/items/itheart.c, ittomato.c, itbox.c, itharisen.c, itlipstick.c, itmariocape.c, itchicorita.c, itthunder.c, and itfushigibana.c. The same diff removed the NonMatching entry for melee/it/items/itchicoritaleaf.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunks around item object list
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible path-fact update for historical match-status changes; curator should verify against current configure.py and PR#2203 before promoting.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that the listed item source files themselves were edited or newly matched in this PR; only configure.py changed in the slice.
Do not treat the bot percentage report as full per-object verification; no objdiff or build log details are loaded.
Do not infer reviewer intent or acceptance rationale; no review comments are present.
Do not promote a global standard from this PR alone; it is a narrow manifest restoration.
